metadata.title:
TO DOUBLE:
PUMPING


CAPACITY

SPOKANE TO HAVE MOST COM
PLETE PUMPING SYSTEM


IN COUNTRY. &

pages.raw_text:
The pumping capacity of the
Spokane water system win almost
be doubled when the pumps called
for In plans just, prepared by Water
Commissioner .aleuterdahl are in.
stalled at the upriver pumping
station.


1t has been said by laymen not
conversant with engineering mat
'ters that they way to get more
water Is to pump more, and to
pump more means the addition OF
more machinery, as every pump in
the present plant was worked to it,
utmost last summer.


Having disposed of the problems
involved in the storage of water az
represented by the four new stand
pipes and the new reservoir, Com
missioner Reuterdahl turned his at
tention to the matter Of getting
pumps to I'LL these receptacles.


The result Of his efforts is the
preparation of one Of the most COM
plete pumping plants of It's ca
pacity to be found In the country
Three new pumps will be installed
and room left for a fourth in the
new building or addition to be
erected at the upriver pumping sta
tion.. The pumps will be Of the turs
bine multtphase centrifugal VAR
ETY., now being installed in tho
most modern pumping plants. The
pumps are directly connected within
electric motors, no belts or shaft
ing OF any kind being employed.
One Of the pumps will be connected
with the city's water power, which
is deemed ample to operate some
of the new pumps In addition to all
of the old ones.


The present capacity of the mp
river pumping station on the low
pressure, such az supplies the
downtown district and all OF the
city between the north and south
hills, is 34,500.000 gallons In "a
hours. Only four of the Is pumps
at present employed con work on
high pressure, pumping to the high
lands, and their capacity 10 10000
900 gallons daily.


The new pumps are constructed
SO that they can work ON either the
high or low pressure. Their ca
pacity in "a hours on the low press.
ure Is 28.e00.000 gallons, and on
the high pressure 1850. 900 gar
Ions. Thus the capacity for pump
ing water to the higher levels viii
be almost trebled.


Bids for these pumps and the
electric motors attached will be
opened October s.
